topic: World History Topics (two, same as SL)
subject: history

items.q: Why did Caribbean migrants come to Britain in 1948 on the Windrush?
items.correct: Britain faced post-war labour shortages; the 1948 British Nationality Act gave Commonwealth citizens UK residency rights and Caribbean migrants answered the call to rebuild.
items.misconception: Windrush migrants were illegal immigrants from outside the empire.
items.random_wrong: Windrush migrants came as refugees from Caribbean famines.
items.why: They arrived as British subjects with full legal rights — the irony of the modern 'Windrush scandal'. Students miss the citizenship framework that made the migration unproblematic in law.

items.q: Why did the Communists win the Chinese Civil War?
items.correct: Mao's appeal to peasants through land reform, the discrediting of the KMT by corruption and inflation, and the disciplined organisation of the PLA — not foreign aid.
items.misconception: Mao won because the USSR sent the Red Army to help.
items.random_wrong: Mao won because the USA refused to support the KMT.
items.why: Soviet aid was limited and often unhelpful; the US heavily backed the KMT. The Communist victory was won domestically through rural mobilisation — a key historiographical point.

items.q: Why did the Soviet Union collapse in 1991?
items.correct: A combination of economic stagnation, nationalist movements in republics, the failed August 1991 coup, and Gorbachev's reforms accelerating loss of control to Yeltsin.
items.misconception: The USSR collapsed because Reagan outspent it in the arms race.
items.random_wrong: The USSR collapsed because NATO invaded Moscow.
items.why: Reagan's arms spending strained but did not topple the USSR; internal economic and political crisis did. Students inherit a triumphalist US narrative that overstates external pressure.

items.q: Which 1938 conference ceded the Sudetenland to Germany?
items.why: It's Munich. “Locarno” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Who signed the 1939 non-aggression pact for the USSR?
items.why: It's Molotov. “Litvinov” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what month and year did Hitler remilitarise the Rhineland?
items.why: Exact answer: 1936 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
